service:
You can use the Billing API to programatically list the billing views available to you for a given time period. A billing view represents a set of billing data.

The Billing API provides the following endpoint:

https://billing.us-east-1.api.aws

operations.AssociateSourceViews: Associates one or more source billing views with an existing billing view. This allows creating aggregate billing views that combine data from multiple sources.
operations.CreateBillingView: Creates a billing view with the specified billing view attributes.
operations.DisassociateSourceViews: Removes the association between one or more source billing views and an existing billing view. This allows modifying the composition of aggregate billing views.
operations.GetBillingPreferences: Retrieves billing preferences for the specified feature. Each feature controls a distinct billing capability: which accounts can share Reserved Instances or credits, whether billing alerts are enabled, the historical record of sharing changes, and per-credit options.
operations.GetBillingView: Returns the metadata associated to the specified billing view ARN.
operations.GetCreditAllocationHistory: Returns the per-billing-month allocation history for credits applied to an Amazon Web Services account's bills. Traverses the consolidated billing family to capture cross-account credit applications. Supports pagination and optional filtering to a single credit.
operations.GetCredits:
Returns the list of Amazon Web Services account credits for the specified account. Each credit includes its identifier, type, monetary amounts, applicable products, expiration, sharing configuration, and current enabled status.

When the caller is the management account of a consolidated billing family and payerAccountFlag is true, the response aggregates credits across the entire family. Otherwise, the response includes only credits owned by the account specified in accountId.
operations.GetResourcePolicy: Returns the resource-based policy document attached to the resource in JSON format.
operations.ListBillingViews:
Lists the billing views available for a given time period.

Every Amazon Web Services account has a unique PRIMARY billing view that represents the billing data available by default. Accounts that use Billing Conductor also have BILLING_GROUP billing views representing pro forma costs associated with each created billing group.
operations.ListSourceViewsForBillingView: Lists the source views (managed Amazon Web Services billing views) associated with the billing view.
operations.ListTagsForResource: Lists tags associated with the billing view resource.
operations.RedeemCredits: Redeems an Amazon Web Services promotional credit code on behalf of the calling account. On success, a new credit is added to the account's credit ledger with the amount, validity period, and applicable products defined by the promotion. The credit is then automatically applied to subsequent bills according to the standard credit application order.
operations.TagResource: An API operation for adding one or more tags (key-value pairs) to a resource.
operations.UntagResource: Removes one or more tags from a resource. Specify only tag keys in your request. Don't specify the value.
operations.UpdateBillingPreferences:
Updates billing preferences for the specified feature. Each feature targets a distinct billing capability and has its own set of supported keys. The action sets the value for each provided key; keys not present in the request are unchanged.

Sharing keys (RI_SHARING, CREDIT_SHARING, CREDIT_LEVEL_SHARING, and sharing keys under CREDIT_PREFERENCE_OPTIONS) may only be set by the management account of a consolidated billing family. The credit/{creditId}/status key may be set by member accounts for credits they own, or by the management account for any credit in the family.
operations.UpdateBillingView: An API to update the attributes of the billing view.

AccessDeniedException.base: You don't have sufficient access to perform this action.

refs.BillingPreferenceSummary$accountId: The associated Amazon Web Services account ID. Populated for account-list keys; null otherwise.
refs.BillingViewElement$ownerAccountId: The account owner of the billing view.
refs.BillingViewElement$sourceAccountId: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the source billing view, if this is a derived billing view.
refs.BillingViewListElement$ownerAccountId: The list of owners of the Billing view.
refs.BillingViewListElement$sourceAccountId: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the source billing view, if this is a derived billing view.
refs.CreditAllocationHistoryEntry$accountId: The Amazon Web Services account the credit was applied to.
refs.CreditData$accountId: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the credit.
refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryRequest$accoun: The Amazon Web Services account ID whose allocation history to retrieve. Must be a 12-digit numeric string.
refs.ListBillingViewsRequest$ownerAccountId: The list of owners of the billing view.
refs.ListBillingViewsRequest$sourceAccountId: Filters the results to include only billing views that use the specified account as a source.

refs.BillingPreferenceSummary$accountName: The display name of the account. Populated together with accountId; null otherwise.

ActiveTimeRange.base: A time range with a start and end time.

refs.ListBillingViewsRequest$activeTimeRange: The time range for the billing views listed. PRIMARY billing view is always listed. BILLING_GROUP billing views are listed for time ranges when the associated billing group resource in Billing Conductor is active. The time range must be within one calendar month.

Amount.base: A monetary amount with a currency code. Used throughout the Billing API to represent credit balances, allocations, and adjustments.

refs.CreditAllocationHistoryEntry$creditAmoun: The amount of credit applied. Negative values represent credits that reduced the bill.
refs.CreditData$initialAmount: The initial amount of the credit when it was issued.
refs.CreditData$estimatedAmount: The estimated remaining balance, including in-flight (open) bills that have not yet been finalized.

refs.CreditData$applicationType: When the credit is applied during bill computation. Valid values: BEFORE_CROSS_SERVICE_DISCOUNTS, AFTER_DISCOUNTS.

refs.UpdateBillingPreferencesRequest$feature: The feature to update. Valid values: BILLING_ALERTS, RI_SHARING, CREDIT_SHARING, CREDIT_LEVEL_SHARING, CREDIT_PREFERENCE_OPTIONS. The history features (RI_SHARING_HISTORY and CREDIT_SHARING_HISTORY) are read-only and cannot be updated.

BillingFeatureFilter.base: A filter that narrows the set of preferences returned by GetBillingPreferences.

refs.BillingFeatureFilter$name: The filter name. Currently the only supported value is PREFERENCE_KEY.

refs.BillingFeatureFilter$value: The filter values to match. For PREFERENCE_KEY, supply 1 to 10 preference key values to match.

refs.CreditAllocationHistoryEntry$billingMont: The billing month of the application in YYYY-MM format.

BillingPeriod.base: A specific billing period identified by year and month.

refs.BillingPreferenceSummary$billingPeriod: The billing period associated with the preference change. Populated only for the history features RI_SHARING_HISTORY and CREDIT_SHARING_HISTORY.

BillingPreferenceForKey.base: A single key/value entry used to update a billing preference.

BillingPreferenceSummary.base: A single billing preference entry returned by GetBillingPreferences.

refs.GetBillingPreferencesResponse$billingPre: The list of preference entries matching the request.

refs.UpdateBillingPreferencesRequest$billingP: Key/value pairs to apply. All keys in a single request must be valid for the specified feature and must not be duplicated. For CREDIT_PREFERENCE_OPTIONS, all keys must reference the same creditId.

refs.AssociateSourceViewsRequest$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the billing view to associate source views with.
refs.AssociateSourceViewsResponse$arn: The ARN of the billing view that the source views were associated with.
refs.BillingViewElement$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.BillingViewListElement$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.CreateBillingViewResponse$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.DeleteBillingViewRequest$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.DeleteBillingViewResponse$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.DisassociateSourceViewsRequest$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the billing view to disassociate source views from.
refs.DisassociateSourceViewsResponse$arn: The ARN of the billing view that the source views were disassociated from.
refs.GetBillingViewRequest$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.ListSourceViewsForBillingViewRequest$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.UpdateBillingViewRequest$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.
refs.UpdateBillingViewResponse$arn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.

refs.ListBillingViewsRequest$arns: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that can be used to uniquely identify the billing view.

refs.BillingViewElement$description: The description of the billing view.
refs.BillingViewListElement$description: The description of the billing view.
refs.CreateBillingViewRequest$description: The description of the billing view.
refs.UpdateBillingViewRequest$description: The description of the billing view.

BillingViewElement.base: The metadata associated to the billing view.

refs.GetBillingViewResponse$billingView: The billing view element associated with the specified ARN.

BillingViewHealthStatus.base: Represents the health status of a billing view, including a status code and optional reasons for the status.

refs.BillingViewElement$healthStatus: The current health status of the billing view.
refs.BillingViewListElement$healthStatus: The current health status of the billing view.

BillingViewHealthStatusException.base: Exception thrown when a billing view's health status prevents an operation from being performed. This may occur if the billing view is in a state other than HEALTHY.

refs.BillingViewElement$name: The account name of the billing view.
refs.BillingViewListElement$name: A list of names of the Billing view.
refs.CreateBillingViewRequest$name: The name of the billing view.
refs.UpdateBillingViewRequest$name: The name of the billing view.

refs.AssociateSourceViewsRequest$sourceViews: A list of ARNs of the source billing views to associate.
refs.CreateBillingViewRequest$sourceViews: A list of billing views used as the data source for the custom billing view.
refs.DisassociateSourceViewsRequest$sourceVie: A list of ARNs of the source billing views to disassociate.
refs.ListSourceViewsForBillingViewResponse$so: A list of billing views used as the data source for the custom billing view.

refs.BillingViewHealthStatus$statusCode: The current health status code of the billing view.

refs.BillingViewHealthStatus$statusReasons: A list of reasons explaining the current health status, if applicable.

refs.ListBillingViewsRequest$maxResults: The maximum number of billing views to retrieve. Default is 100.
refs.ListSourceViewsForBillingViewRequest$max: The number of entries a paginated response contains.

refs.BillingPeriod$year: The four-digit year of the billing period.

refs.CreditAllocationHistoryEntry$isEstimated: true when the entry was applied to an in-flight bill that has not yet been finalized.
refs.CreditData$accountHasCreditSharingEnable: Whether the owning account has account-level credit sharing turned on.
refs.DeleteBillingViewRequest$force: If set to true, forces deletion of the billing view even if it has derived resources (e.g. other billing views or budgets). Use with caution as this may break dependent resources.
refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryResponse$parti: true when data could not be retrieved for one or more billing months. The failedMonths field lists which months are missing.
refs.GetCreditsRequest$payerAccountFlag: When true and the caller is the management account, the response aggregates credits across the entire consolidated billing family. When false or omitted, returns only credits for the specified accountId.

refs.CreateBillingViewRequest$clientToken: A unique, case-sensitive identifier you specify to ensure idempotency of the request. Idempotency ensures that an API request completes no more than one time. If the original request completes successfully, any subsequent retries complete successfully without performing any further actions with an idempotent request.

ConflictException.base: The requested operation would cause a conflict with the current state of a service resource associated with the request. Resolve the conflict before retrying this request.

refs.CostCategoryValues$key: The unique name of the Cost Category.

CostCategoryValues.base: The Cost Categories values used for filtering the costs.

refs.Expression$costCategories: The filter that's based on CostCategory values.

CreditAllocationHistoryEntry.base: A single entry in the credit allocation history, representing how a credit was applied to a specific service during a billing month.

refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryResponse$credi: Allocation entries sorted by billingMonth in descending order.

CreditData.base: Detailed information about an Amazon Web Services credit, including its identifier, type, monetary amounts, applicable products, sharing configuration, and current enabled status.

refs.GetCreditsResponse$credits: The list of credits matching the request. Returns an empty list when no credits exist.

refs.CreditAllocationHistoryEntry$creditId: The identifier of the credit that was applied.

refs.CreditData$creditSharingType: The sharing configuration for the credit. Valid values: DEFAULT, DISABLED, CUSTOM, COST_CATEGORY_RULE.

refs.CreditData$creditStatus: Whether the credit participates in billing runs. Valid values: ENABLED, DISABLED.

refs.Amount$currencyAmount: The amount as a decimal string (for example, "743.21"). Negative values represent credits that reduce a bill.

refs.Amount$currencyCode: The ISO 4217 currency code for the amount (for example, USD).

refs.DimensionValues$key: The names of the metadata types that you can use to filter and group your results.

DimensionValues.base: The metadata that you can use to filter and group your results.

refs.ValidationExceptionField$message: The message describing why the field failed validation.

Expression.base: See Expression. Billing view only supports LINKED_ACCOUNT, Tags, and CostCategories.

refs.BillingViewElement$dataFilterExpression: See Expression. Billing view only supports LINKED_ACCOUNT, Tags, and CostCategories.
refs.CreateBillingViewRequest$dataFilterExpre: See Expression. Billing view only supports LINKED_ACCOUNT, Tags, and CostCategories.

refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryResponse$faile: Billing months in YYYY-MM format that failed to return data. Non-empty only when partialResults is true.

refs.GetBillingPreferencesRequest$features: The feature to retrieve. Specify exactly one value. Valid values: BILLING_ALERTS, RI_SHARING, RI_SHARING_HISTORY, CREDIT_SHARING, CREDIT_SHARING_HISTORY, CREDIT_LEVEL_SHARING, CREDIT_PREFERENCE_OPTIONS.

refs.GetBillingPreferencesRequest$filters: Filters to narrow results. Specify exactly one filter when supplied. The supported filter name is PREFERENCE_KEY, which accepts 1 to 10 values to match preference keys.

refs.GetBillingPreferencesRequest$maxResults: The maximum number of records to return per page. Range: 1 to 50. Default: 50.

refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryRequest$maxRes: The maximum number of records to return per page. Range: 1 to 1000. Default: 100.

refs.BillingViewElement$derivedViewCount: The number of billing views that use this billing view as a source.
refs.BillingViewElement$sourceViewCount: The number of source views associated with this billing view.

InternalServerException.base: The request processing failed because of an unknown error, exception, or failure.

refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryRequest$credit: Filters the result to a single credit. When omitted, returns allocation entries for all credits.

refs.BillingPeriod$month: The month of the billing period as an integer between 1 and 12.

refs.GetBillingPreferencesRequest$nextToken: Pagination token from a previous response. Pass the value returned in nextToken to retrieve the next page of results.
refs.GetBillingPreferencesResponse$nextToken: Pagination token. Present when more pages are available; null when there are no more results.
refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryRequest$nextTo: Pagination token from a previous response. Pass the value returned in nextToken to retrieve the next page of results.
refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryResponse$nextT: Pagination token. Present when more pages are available; null when there are no more results.
refs.ListBillingViewsRequest$nextToken: The pagination token that is used on subsequent calls to list billing views.
refs.ListBillingViewsResponse$nextToken: The pagination token to use on subsequent calls to list billing views.
refs.ListSourceViewsForBillingViewRequest$nex: The pagination token that is used on subsequent calls to list billing views.
refs.ListSourceViewsForBillingViewResponse$ne: The pagination token that is used on subsequent calls to list billing views.

refs.GetResourcePolicyResponse$policy: The resource-based policy document attached to the resource in JSON format.

refs.BillingPreferenceForKey$key: The preference key. Format depends on the feature being updated.
refs.BillingPreferenceSummary$key: The preference key. Format depends on the feature.

refs.CreditData$applicableProductNames: The names of Amazon Web Services services this credit applies to.

refs.CreditData$purchaseTypeApplications: Restricts which purchase types this credit applies to. When null or omitted, the credit applies to all purchase types.

refs.GetResourcePolicyRequest$resourceArn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the billing view resource to which the policy is attached to.
refs.GetResourcePolicyResponse$resourceArn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the billing view resource to which the policy is attached to.
refs.ListTagsForResourceRequest$resourceArn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource.
refs.TagResourceRequest$resourceArn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource.
refs.UntagResourceRequest$resourceArn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource.

refs.ConflictException$resourceId: The identifier for the service resource associated with the request.
refs.ResourceNotFoundException$resourceId: Value is a list of resource IDs that were not found.

ResourceNotFoundException.base: The specified ARN in the request doesn't exist.

ResourceTag.base: The tag structure that contains a tag key and value.

refs.ResourceTag$key: The key that's associated with the tag.

refs.UntagResourceRequest$resourceTagKeys: A list of tag key value pairs that are associated with the resource.

refs.CreateBillingViewRequest$resourceTags: A list of key value map specifying tags associated to the billing view being created.
refs.ListTagsForResourceResponse$resourceTags: A list of tag key value pairs that are associated with the resource.
refs.TagResourceRequest$resourceTags: A list of tag key value pairs that are associated with the resource.

refs.ResourceTag$value: The value that's associated with the tag.

refs.ConflictException$resourceType: The type of resource associated with the request.
refs.ResourceNotFoundException$resourceType: Value is the type of resource that was not found.
refs.ServiceQuotaExceededException$resourceTy: The type of Amazon Web Services resource.

refs.StringSearch$searchOption: The type of search operation to perform on the string value. Determines how the search value is matched against the target field.

refs.StringSearch$searchValue: The string value to use in the search operation. This value is compared against the target field using the specified search option.

ServiceQuotaExceededException.base: You've reached the limit of resources you can create, or exceeded the size of an individual resource.

refs.CreditData$shareableAccounts: The Amazon Web Services account IDs entitled to apply this credit.

refs.CreditAllocationHistoryEntry$description: A human-readable description of the credit allocation.
refs.CreditAllocationHistoryEntry$appliedServ: The Amazon Web Services service the credit was applied to.
refs.CreditData$creditConsoleVisibility: The display configuration for the credit in the Amazon Web Services Billing console.
refs.CreditData$costCategoryArn: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Cost Category controlling the credit's sharing scope. Present only when creditSharingType is COST_CATEGORY_RULE.
refs.CreditData$ruleName: The rule name within the Cost Category. Present only when creditSharingType is COST_CATEGORY_RULE.
refs.GetCreditsRequest$accountId: The Amazon Web Services account ID. Must be a 12-digit numeric string.

StringSearch.base: A structure that defines how to search for string values. You can specify a search option and the value to search for.

refs.ListBillingViewsRequest$names: Filters the list of billing views by name. You can specify search criteria to match billing view names based on the search option provided.

TagValues.base: The values that are available for a tag.

ThrottlingException.base: The request was denied due to request throttling.

TimeRange.base: Specifies a time range with inclusive begin and end dates.

refs.Expression$timeRange: Specifies a time range filter for the billing view data.

refs.ActiveTimeRange$activeBeforeInclusive: The inclusive time range end date.
refs.BillingViewElement$createdAt: The time when the billing view was created.
refs.BillingViewElement$updatedAt: The time when the billing view was last updated.
refs.BillingViewElement$viewDefinitionLastUpd: The timestamp of when the billing view definition was last updated.
refs.CreateBillingViewResponse$createdAt: The time when the billing view was created.
refs.CreditData$startDate: The date the credit becomes valid, as Unix epoch seconds.
refs.CreditData$endDate: The date the credit expires, as Unix epoch seconds.
refs.CreditData$exhaustDate: The date the credit balance reached zero, as Unix epoch seconds.
refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryRequest$startD: Inclusive start date as Unix epoch seconds. Must be on or before endDate. The range from startDate to endDate cannot exceed 24 billing months.
refs.GetCreditAllocationHistoryRequest$endDat: Inclusive end date as Unix epoch seconds.
refs.GetCreditsRequest$startDate: The start date for the credit period as Unix epoch seconds. Must be a past date that is not more than one year before the current date.
refs.GetCreditsRequest$endDate: The end date for the credit period as Unix epoch seconds. Must not be a future date and must be on or after startDate. Defaults to the current date when omitted.
refs.TimeRange$beginDateInclusive: The inclusive start date of the time range.
refs.TimeRange$endDateInclusive: The inclusive end date of the time range.
refs.UpdateBillingViewResponse$updatedAt: The time when the billing view was last updated.

ValidationException.base: The input fails to satisfy the constraints specified by an Amazon Web Services service.

ValidationExceptionField.base: The field's information of a request that resulted in an exception.

refs.ValidationException$fieldList: The input fails to satisfy the constraints specified by an Amazon Web Services service.

refs.ValidationException$reason: The input fails to satisfy the constraints specified by an Amazon Web Services service.

refs.CostCategoryValues$values: The specific value of the Cost Category.
refs.DimensionValues$values: The metadata values that you can use to filter and group your results.
refs.TagValues$values: The specific value of the tag.
